validation_results.test_output_summary: 8 passed in 0.60s - all tests for projects resource passed including listing, degradation, deduplication, registration, JSON format, and URI contract
validation_results.code_quality: All files pass ruff check and format with zero errors

criteria_verification.notes: src/fleet_memory/mcp/resources.py exists with register_projects_resource() function using @mcp.resource('memory://projects') decorator. server.py register_all() calls it. Independent tests test_resource_registration and test_projects_resource_uri passed.

Implementation uses list_namespaces(prefix=('fleet_memory',), max_depth=2) to extract project names from namespace tuples. Test test_lists_projects_with_memories passed, verifying multiple projects are returned.
Listing derived dynamically from AsyncPostgresStore.list_namespaces() via set comprehension {ns[1] for ns in namespaces if len(ns) >= 2}. No hardcoded project lists present.

@tool_safe decorator applied to _read_projects() providing structured degradation. Tests test_store_down_degrades and test_store_none_degrades both passed, confirming graceful error handling with JSON error envelope instead of crashes.
All modified files (src/fleet_memory/mcp/resources.py, tests/unit/test_mcp_projects_resource.py, src/fleet_memory/mcp/server.py) passed ruff check and ruff format --check with zero errors.

rationale: All five acceptance criteria verified through independent test execution (8/8 passed), requirements evidence, and quality gates. Honesty verification shows no discrepancies (score 1.0). Implementation correctly uses list_namespaces() for dynamic project discovery, applies tool_safe for graceful degradation, integrates with TASK-MCP-001 extension point, and passes all lint/format checks. Edge cases for store failures, empty stores, and deduplication are all tested and passing.
